content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
May 30, 1997
The Honorable Steve Baldwin
Assembly Member, California Legislature
State Capitol
Post Office Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-97-241
Dear Assembly Member Baldwin:
This letter is a response to your inquiry regarding the provisions ofthe Political Reform
Act (the “Act”).’
Your request involves potential conflicts of interest by a third party, El Cajon
City Councilmember Bob McClellan.
We cannot render advice to a third party who is not authorized to request written advice
on behalf of the public official whose duties are in question. (Regulation 18329(c), copy
enclosed.) Consequently, we can only provide you general guidance regarding the issue that
your raise in your letter. This letter should not be construed to evaluate the legal opinion ofthe
city attorney of the City of El Cajon.
Essentially, you ask what the applicable time period for disqualification is under Section
87 103(c) with respect to sources of income to a public official totaling $250 or more.
Section 87103(c) states:
“Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans
by a commercial lending institution in the regular course ofbusiness
on terms available to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to, received by or promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made.”
Government Code Sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, Sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916)322-5660


File No. 0-97-24 1
Page 2
As set forth in Section 87 103(c), the applicable time period for disqualification with
respect to a person who has been a source of income to a public official is the 12 months prior to
the time a decision is made.
To further assist you in your inquiry, we have enclosed copies of two letters which may
be helpful to you and an informational pamphlet concerning the conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Act. (Copies enclosed.)
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
5L ‘-‘--
By:
LuisaMenchaca
Senior Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
SGC:LM:ak

sections.question: What is the applicable time period for disqualification under Section 87103(c) with respect to sources of income to a public official totaling $250 or more?
sections.conclusion: As set forth in Section 87103(c), the applicable time period for disqualification with respect to a person who has been a source of income to a public official is the 12 months prior to the time a decision is made.
sections.question_synthetic: Assembly Member Baldwin seeks guidance on the disqualification time period under Section 87103(c) of the Political Reform Act regarding income sources of $250 or more for a third party city councilmember.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the relevant time period for disqualification is 12 months prior to when a decision is made, as specified in Section 87103(c).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains clear Q&A structure. FPPC notes they can only provide general guidance since the requester is not authorized to request advice on behalf of the third party. File number listed inconsistently as G-97-241 in letter header and 0-97-241 on page 2.

QUESTION: What is the applicable time period for disqualification under Section 87103(c) with respect to sources of income to a public official totaling $250 or more?

CONCLUSION: As set forth in Section 87103(c), the applicable time period for disqualification with respect to a person who has been a source of income to a public official is the 12 months prior to the time a decision is made.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel responds to Assembly Member Baldwin's inquiry about conflict of interest provisions for a third party El Cajon City Councilmember, clarifying that the applicable disqualification time period under Section 87103(c) is 12 months prior to the decision being made.
